meaning: adj.伊丽莎白一世时代的,伊丽莎白女王的n.伊丽莎白王朝的人
examples: Lady Gaga wore this tuxedo-ish number, with what looks like portions of an Elizabethan neck ruff protruding from the jacket.
Most people of the Elizabethan times did not continue beyond grammar school, so Shakespeare did have at least an average education.
For all its contemporary feel, this outdoor staging is very much in the Elizabethan spirit.
Looney, who in 1920 proposed an Elizabethan courtier, Edward de Vere, 17th Earl of Oxford.
